page: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local instance storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the r7a.xlarge demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core execution, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks (256 MB), which registers in the bottom 25% at 37.49 GB/sec, alongside weaker performance in medium-sized (7B) LLM inference tasks.

Given its high DDR5 memory density relative to compute resources, the r7a.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high-speed baseline networking. It is well-suited for database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, text processing, and general-purpose memory-intensive applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, though memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is relatively weak. The r7a.xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, in-memory caching, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, designed for database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 AMD EPYC cores, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Features a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for database operations and in-memory caching
